methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
BotBuilt is a Seed-stage construction-technology company that has raised $12.4M to date. The Housing & Construction sector is represented by a sparse comparable universe: two private peers (ICON and Apis Cor) exist, but neither shares sufficient overlap in stage or valuation tier to support a robust peer-median approach. No public construction-tech comparables were identified.

Given data constraints, valuation relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised ($12.4M) as a proxy for current implied value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement yields a $31M implied valuation. This reflects the expected value accretion associated with progressing from Seed to Series A funding round and demonstrating product-market fit validation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M to $36M, with $31M as the median point. This narrow range reflects methodological limitation rather than precision: reliance on a single triangulation method reduces confidence relative to multi-method consensus. Key uncertainties include BotBuilt's burn rate, current cash position, product maturity relative to stage peers, and market adoption trajectory in the Construction vertical. A peer-median approach becomes viable once Housing & Construction comparables with comparable stage and funding profile emerge or once sufficient sector-agnostic Seed-stage data is incorporated.
